rationale: The citing paper appears to cite Sun, Neslin, and Srinivasan (2003) only once as an example of a model that uses discrete time in intertemporal choice modeling. The citation is used to contrast that approach with the authors' continuous-time specification, not to build their core theory, hypotheses, or empirical design around the cited work. The cited paper's promotion-switching framework is not otherwise engaged in the analysis or interpretation, so the role is a brief methodological mention.
